sourceMovie.title: Every Day
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Every Day for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Michael Sucsy keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Age of Adaline
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Every Day clicked for you, The Age of Adaline (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Stardust
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Every Day clicked for you, Stardust (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Every Day?
faq.answer: Try The Age of Adaline, Stardust, Beastly—each shares DNA with Every Day while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Every Day?
faq.answer: Every Day (2018) is best described as Romance, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Every Day worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
